zh.lifeSummary: 當Franklin Vinton於1817年6月出生在美國麻薩諸塞州普萊姆斯布利其沃特時，他的父親Abel Vinton32歲，母親Betsey Merrick25歲。他於1837年10月17日與Olive Rarick在美國俄亥俄州奧加湯普森結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他於1860年住在美國密西根州貝里恩維薩福 堂史普且於1865年住在美國密西根州。他去世於1869年12月9日在美國密西根州貝里恩加利 堂史普加利享年52歲，埋葬在美國密西根州貝里恩維薩福 堂史普格倫多拉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Franklin Vinton于1817年6月出生在美国马萨诸塞州普萊姆斯布利其沃特时，他的父亲Abel Vinton32岁，母亲Betsey Merrick25岁。他于1837年10月17日与Olive Rarick在美国俄亥俄州奧加湯普森结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他于1860年住在美国密歇根州貝里恩維薩福 堂史普且于1865年住在美国密歇根州。他去世于1869年12月9日在美国密歇根州貝里恩加利 堂史普加利享年52岁，埋葬在美国密歇根州貝里恩維薩福 堂史普格倫多拉。

nameMeanings.name: Vinton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of   Finton   with voicing of the initial consonant (see   Fenton  ). In Sussex and Kent the name was probably confused with   Winton  .

variants.name: Finton
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Fenton  .

nameMeanings.name: Franklin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, derived from Middle English frankeleyn ‘freeman’, denoting a member of a class of men who were not of noble birth but who were nevertheless freeholders. The vocabulary word is derived from Old French franc, meaning both ‘free’ and ‘Frankish’. The connection between freemen and Franks is reflected in the Late Latin term francalia, originally denoting lands held by Franks, which came to mean lands not subject to taxes. The given name is now quite common, especially in the United States, where it is often bestowed in honour of the statesman and scientist Benjamin Franklin ( 1706–90 ). A more recent influence was President Franklin D. Roosevelt ( 1882–1945 ).

variants.name: Franklyn
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, derived from Middle English frankeleyn ‘freeman’, denoting a member of a class of men who were not of noble birth but who were nevertheless freeholders. The vocabulary word is derived from Old French franc, meaning both ‘free’ and ‘Frankish’. The connection between freemen and Franks is reflected in the Late Latin term francalia, originally denoting lands held by Franks, which came to mean lands not subject to taxes. The given name is now quite common, especially in the United States, where it is often bestowed in honour of the statesman and scientist Benjamin Franklin ( 1706–90 ). A more recent influence was President Franklin D. Roosevelt ( 1882–1945 ).

historicEvents.title: Panic! of 1819
historicEvents.description: With the Aftermath of the Napoleonic Wars the global market for trade was down. During this time, America had its first financial crisis and it lasted for only two years.

historicEvents.title: State Capital Moves to Indianapolis
historicEvents.description: The state capital was moved from Corydon to Indianapolis on January 10, 1825.

historicEvents.title: The Black Hawk War
historicEvents.description: Convinced that a group of Native American tribes were hostile, The United States formed a frontier militia to stop them in their tracks. Even though Black Hawk was hoping to avoid bloodshed while trying to resettle on tribal land, U.S. officials opened fire on the Native Americans. Black Hawk then responded to this confrontation by successfully attacking the militia at the Battle of Stillman's Run and then left northward. After a few months the militia caught up with Black Hawk and his men and defeated them at the Battle of Wisconsin Heights. While being weakened by hunger, injuries and desertion, Black Hawk and the rest of the many native survivors retreated towards the Mississippi. Unfortunately, Black Hawk and other leaders were later captured when they surrendered to the US forces and were then imprisoned for a year.
Historical Boundaries 1832:  Wabash, Indiana, United States
U.S. acquires vast tracts of Mexican territory in wake of Mexican War including California and New Mexico.

historicEvents.title: Moving the State Capital
historicEvents.description: The capital of Michigan was moved from Detroit to Lansing on March 17, 1847. The capital was moved to be further away from Canada, to encourage settlement and boost economy toward the inner regions of the state, and to make to capital more accessible to everyone statewide.

historicEvents.title: Ohio supports the Union side of the Civil War
historicEvents.description: Although divided as a state on the subject of slavery, Ohio participated in the Civil War on the Union's side, providing over 300,000 troops. Ohio provided the 3rd largest number of troops by any Union state.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Ohio supports the Union side of the Civil War

historicEvents.description: Abraham Lincoln issues Emancipation Proclamation, declaring slaves in Confederate states to be free.

en.lifeSummary: When Franklin Vinton was born in June 1817, in Bridgewater, Plymouth, Massachusetts, United States, his father, Abel Vinton, was 32 and his mother, Betsey Merrick, was 25. He married Olive Rarick on 17 October 1837, in Thompson, Geauga, Ohio, United States. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 3 daughters. He lived in Weesaw Township, Berrien, Michigan, United States in 1860 and Michigan, United States in 1865. He died on 9 December 1869, in Galien, Galien Township, Berrien, Michigan, United States, at the age of 52, and was buried in Glendora, Weesaw Township, Berrien, Michigan, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Franklin Vinton est né en juin 1817, à Bridgewater, Plymouth, Massachusetts, États-Unis, son père, Abel Vinton, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Betsey Merrick, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Olive Rarick le 17 octobre 1837, à Thompson, Geauga, Ohio, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 3 filles. Il a vécu à Weesaw Township, Berrien, Michigan, États-Unis en 1860 et à Michigan, États-Unis en 1865. Il est décédé le 9 décembre 1869, à Galien, Galien Township, Berrien, Michigan, États-Unis, à l’âge de 52 ans, et a été enterré à Glendora, Weesaw Township, Berrien, Michigan, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Franklin Vinton im Juni 1817 in Bridgewater, Plymouth, Massachusetts, Vereinigte Staaten geboren wurde, war sein Vater Abel Vinton 32 und seine Mutter Betsey Merrick 25. Er hat am 17. Oktober 1837 in Thompson, Geauga, Ohio, Vereinigte Staaten Olive Rarick geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 3 Töchter. 1860 hat er in Weesaw Township, Berrien, Michigan, Vereinigte Staaten und 1865 in Michigan, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt. Er starb am 9. Dezember 1869 im Alter von 52 Jahren in Galien, Galien Township, Berrien, Michigan, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in Glendora, Weesaw Township, Berrien, Michigan, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Franklin Vinton è nato il nel giugno 1817 nel Bridgewater, Plymouth, Massachusetts, Stati Uniti d'America, il suo padre, Abel Vinton, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Betsey Merrick, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Olive Rarick il 17 ottobre 1837, nel Thompson, Geauga, Ohio, Stati Uniti d'America. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 3 figlie. Vissuto a Weesaw Township, Berrien, Michigan, Stati Uniti d'America nel 1860 e nel Michigan, Stati Uniti d'America nel 1865. Morì il 9 dicembre 1869, nel Galien, Galien Township, Berrien, Michigan, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 52 anni, e fu sepolto a Glendora, Weesaw Township, Berrien, Michigan, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Franklin Vintonが1817年6月にアメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州プリモウスブリジェワターで生まれた時，父Abel Vintonは32歳，母Betsey Merrickは25歳であった。1837年10月17日にアメリカ合衆国オハイオ州ジェアウガソンプソンでOlive Rarickと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1860年にはアメリカ合衆国ミシガン州バーリーンウィーサウ トウンシュイップに，1865年にはアメリカ合衆国ミシガン州に住んでいた。彼は1869年12月9日にアメリカ合衆国ミシガン州バーリーンガリーン トウンシュイップガリーンで52歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国ミシガン州バーリーンウィーサウ トウンシュイップグレンドラにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Franklin Vinton(이)가 1817년 6월 미국 매사추세츠 주 플림우스 브리지왓어에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Abel Vinton(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Betsey Merrick(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1837년 10월 17일 미국 오하이오 주 그이가 톰프슨에서 Olive Rarick(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 1860년도에 미국 미시간 주 베리 위사w 타운시프에서, 1865년도에 미국 미시간 주에서 살았다. 그는 1869년 12월 9일 미국 미시간 주 베리 갈리 타운시프 갈리에서 52세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 미시간 주 베리 위사w 타운시프 글렌도라에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Franklin Vinton nasceu em junho de 1817, em Bridgewater, Plymouth, Massachusetts, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, Abel Vinton, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Betsey Merrick, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Olive Rarick em 17 de outubro de 1837, em Thompson, Geauga, Ohio, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 3 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Franklin Vinton родился июнь 1817 г. в местности бриджюэитир, плимоут, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени Abel Vinton было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Betsey Merrick, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Olive Rarick 17 октября 1837 г. в местности томпсон, джога, Огайо, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он жил в местности висав тоуншип, бирриен, Мичиган, Соединенные штаты в 1860 году и в местности Мичиган, Соединенные штаты в 1865 году. Он умер 9 декабря 1869 г. в местности галиен, галиен тоуншип, бирриен, Мичиган, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 52 лет и был похоронен в местности глендора, висав тоуншип, бирриен, Мичиган, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Franklin Vinton nació en junio de 1817, en Bridgewater, Plymouth, Masachusets, Estados Unidos, su papá, Abel Vinton, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Betsey Merrick, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Olive Rarick el 17 de octubre de 1837, en Thompson, Geauga, Ohio, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 3 hijas. Vivió en Weesaw Township, Berrien, Michigan, Estados Unidos en 1860 y en Michigan, Estados Unidos en 1865. Murió el 9 de diciembre de 1869, en Galien, Galien Township, Berrien, Michigan, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 52 años, y fue sepultado en Glendora, Weesaw Township, Berrien, Michigan, Estados Unidos.
